courseleaf.title: Greek, Minor
courseleaf.informationtext: The Department offers minors in Classical Civilization, Greek and Latin for those wishing to follow a shorter but formally recognized program of study in Classics.
courseleaf.requirementstext:
Course List
Code Title Units
Choose one:4
CLA 001
Ancient Near East & Early Greece: 3000-500 B.C.E.
CLA 002
Ancient Greece & the Near East: 500-146 B.C.E.
Three upper division courses in Greek 112
Greek (GRK)
One additional upper division course in Classics, Greek, or Latin.4
Classics (CLA)
Greek (GRK)
Latin (LAT)
Total Units20
1

Upper division language courses require completion of the lower division language series or equivalent work as approved by the department.
courseleaf.quickviewtext: What better way to fully enjoy such seminal texts from Homer to the New Testament than to read them in the original language? Greek has strongly influenced other languages, English included, and its cultural heritage is fundamental to many modern fields of study. Students in the Greek minor improve their comprehension of English grammar and acquire excellent verbal and written communication skills. Approximately 65% of English vocabulary and 90% of technical & scientific terminology are derived from Greek and Latin.
